sectiondef.header: Packet Register bit definitions and masks
Memory Address Register 1 bit definitions and masks
Memory Address Register 2 bit definitions and masks
Command Register bit definitions and masks
Program Register bit definitions and masks
Interrupt Status Enable Register bit definitions and masks

briefdescription.para: Single Bit Error Status Enable, BCH Detect Error Status Enable.

sectiondef.header: Interrupt Signal Enable Register bit definitions and masks

briefdescription.para: Single Bit Error Signal Enable, BCH Detect Error Signal Enable.

sectiondef.header: Interrupt Status Register bit definitions and masks

briefdescription.para: Single Bit Error, BCH Detect Error.
Single Bit Error Status Enable, BCH Detect Error Status Enable.

sectiondef.header: ID2 Register bit definitions and masks
Flash Status Register bit definitions and masks
Timing Register bit definitions and masks

briefdescription.para: Address latch enable to Data loading time.

sectiondef.header: ECC Register bit definitions and masks
ECC Error Count Register bit definitions and masks
ECC Spare Command Register bit definitions and masks

briefdescription.para: Number of ECC/ spare address cycles.

sectiondef.header: Data Interface Register bit definitions and masks
DMA Buffer Boundary Register bit definitions and masks
Slave DMA Configuration Register bit definitions and masks

briefdescription.para: The XNandPsu_DataInterface enum contains flash operating mode.
The XNandPsu_SWMode enum contains the driver operating mode.
The XNandPsu_DmaMode enum contains the controller MDMA mode.
The XNandPsu_EccMode enum contains ECC functionality.
This enum defines the ONFI 3.1 commands.

memberdef.name: EccMatrix

briefdescription.para: Each XNandPsu device in the system has an entry in this table.
Each XNandPsu device in the system has an entry in this table.
This function initializes the NAND flash and gets the geometry information.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPsu instance.

briefdescription.para: This function initializes the geometry information from ONFI parameter page.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPsu instance.
is pointer to the ONFI parameter page.

briefdescription.para: This function initializes the feature list from ONFI parameter page.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPsu instance.
is pointer to ONFI parameter page buffer.

briefdescription.para: This function initializes the Data Interface from ONFI parameter page.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPsu instance.
is pointer to ONFI parameter page buffer.

briefdescription.para: This function initializes the Timing mode from ONFI parameter page.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPsu instance.
is pointer to ONFI parameter page buffer.

memberdef.argsstring: (XNandPsu *InstancePtr, u32 RegOffset, u32 Mask, u32 Timeout)
memberdef.name: XNandPsu_PollRegTimeout

briefdescription.para: This function polls for a register bit set status till the timeout.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPsu instance.
is the offset of register.
is the bitmask.
is the timeout value.

briefdescription.para: This function sets packet size and packet count values in packet register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPsu instance.
is the packet size.
is the packet count.

briefdescription.para: This function sets Page and Column values in the Memory address registers.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPsu instance.
is the page value.
is the column value.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the size of page in Command Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPsu instance.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the chip select value in memory address2 register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPsu instance.
is the chip select value.

briefdescription.para: This function sends ONFI Reset command to the flash.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPsu instance.
is the chip select value.

briefdescription.para: This function sends ONFI Read Status command to the flash.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPsu instance.
is the chip select value.
is the ONFI status value to return.

memberdef.argsstring: (XNandPsu *InstancePtr, u32 Target, u8 IdAddr, u32 IdLen, u8 *Buf)

briefdescription.para: This function sends ONFI Read ID command to the flash.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPsu instance.
is the chip select value.
is the ONFI ID value to return.

briefdescription.para: This function sends the ONFI Read Parameter Page command to flash.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPsu instance.
is the chip select value.
is the index of parameter page.
is the parameter page information to return.

memberdef.argsstring: (XNandPsu *InstancePtr, u32 Target, u32 Page, u32 Col, u8 *Buf)

briefdescription.para: This function sends ONFI Program Page command to flash.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPsu instance.
is the chip select value.
is the page address value to program.
is the column address value to program.
is the data buffer to program.

memberdef.argsstring: (XNandPsu *InstancePtr, u32 Target, u32 Page, u32 Col, u8 *Buf)
memberdef.name: XNandPsu_ReadPage

briefdescription.para: This function sends ONFI Read Page command to flash.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPsu instance.
is the chip select value.
is the page address value to read.
is the column address value to read.
is the data buffer to fill in.

briefdescription.para: This function checks if the flash supports on-die ECC.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPsu instance.
is pointer to ONFI parameter page.

briefdescription.para: This function setup the Ecc Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPsu instance.

memberdef.argsstring: (XNandPsu *InstancePtr, u32 Target, u32 Col, u32 PktSize, u32 PktCount, u8 *Buf)
memberdef.name: XNandPsu_ChangeReadColumn

briefdescription.para: This function issues change read column and reads the data into buffer specified by user.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPsu instance.
is the chip select value.
is the coulmn address.
is the number of bytes to read.
is the number of transactions to read.
is the data buffer to fill in.

briefdescription.para: This function issues change read column and reads the data into buffer specified by user.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPsu instance.
is the chip select value.
is the coulmn address.
is the number of bytes to read.
is the number of transactions to read.
is the data buffer to fill in.

briefdescription.para: This function initializes extended parameter page ECC information.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPsu instance.
is the Extended parameter page buffer.

memberdef.argsstring: (XNandPsu *InstancePtr, u8 *Buf, u32 PktCount, u32 PktSize, u32 Operation, u8 DmaMode)
memberdef.name: XNandPsu_Data_ReadWrite

briefdescription.para: This function Read/Writes data from the nand controller.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPsu instance.
is the data buffer.
is the number packet chunks.
is the size of the packet.
is 1 for write and 0 for read.
is 1 for Dma and 0 for PIO.

briefdescription.para: This function waits for the transfer complete event.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPsu instance.

simplesect.para: Expects that transfer complete event was set before calling this function.

briefdescription.para: This function waits for the device ready stataus.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPsu instance.
is the chipselect value.

briefdescription.para: This function reads data from the fifo.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPsu instance.
is the buffer pointer.
of the Buffer.

briefdescription.para: This function writes data to the fifo.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPsu instance.
is the buffer pointer.
of the Buffer.

briefdescription.para: This function configures the given dma address to the controller.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPsu instance.
is the buffer pointer.

briefdescription.para: This function initializes a specific XNandPsu instance.

detaileddescription.para: This function must be called prior to using the NAND flash device to read or write any data.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPsu instance.
points to XNandPsu device configuration structure.
is the base address of NAND flash controller.

simplesect.para: The user needs to first call the XNandPsu_LookupConfig() API which returns the Configuration structure pointer which is passed as a parameter to the XNandPsu_CfgInitialize() API.

briefdescription.para: This function enables DMA mode of controller operation.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPsu instance.

briefdescription.para: This function disables DMA mode of driver/controller operation.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPsu instance.

briefdescription.para: This function enables ECC mode of driver/controller operation.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPsu instance.

briefdescription.para: This function disables ECC mode of driver/controller operation.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPsu instance.

briefdescription.para: This function setup the Ecc Spare Command Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPsu instance.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the length including bad blocks from a given offset and length.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the XNandPsu instance.
is the flash data address to read from.
is number of bytes to read.

listitem.para: Return actual length including bad blocks.

briefdescription.para: This function writes to the flash.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPsu instance.
is the starting offset of flash to write.
is the number of bytes to write.
is the source data buffer to write.

briefdescription.para: This function reads from the flash.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPsu instance.
is the starting offset of flash to read.
is the number of bytes to read.
is the destination data buffer to fill in.
is a pointer to the XNandPsu instance.
is the starting offset of flash to erase.
is the number of bytes to erase.

simplesect.para: The Offset and Length should be aligned to block size boundary to get better results.

briefdescription.para: This function sends ONFI Program Page command to flash.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPsu instance.
is the page address value to program.
is the data buffer to program.

briefdescription.para: This function reads spare bytes from flash.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPsu instance.
is the page address value to read.
is the data buffer to fill in.

briefdescription.para: This function sends ONFI block erase command to the flash.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPsu instance.
is the chip select value.
is the block to erase.

briefdescription.para: This function sends ONFI Get Feature command to flash.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPsu instance.
is the chip select value.
is the feature selector.
is the buffer to fill feature value.

briefdescription.para: This function sends ONFI Set Feature command to flash.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPsu instance.
is the chip select value.
is the feature selector.
is the feature value to send.

briefdescription.para: This function changes clock frequency of flash controller.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPsu instance.
is the clock frequency to change.

briefdescription.para: This function changes the data interface and timing mode.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPsu instance.
is the new data interface.
is the new timing mode.

memberdef.argsstring: (XNandPsu *InstancePtr, u8 Cmd1, u8 Cmd2, u8 EccState, u8 DmaMode, u8 AddrCycles)
memberdef.name: XNandPsu_Prepare_Cmd

briefdescription.para: This function prepares command to be written into command register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPsu instance.
is the first Onfi Command.
is the second Onfi Command.
is the flag to set Ecc State.
is the flag to set DMA mode.
is the number of Address Cycles.

briefdescription.para: This function reads the Bad Block Table(BBT) if present in flash.

detaileddescription.para: If not it scans the flash for detecting factory marked bad blocks and creates a bad block table and write the Bad Block Table(BBT) into the flash.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPsu instance.

briefdescription.para: This function marks a block as bad in the RAM based Bad Block Table(BBT).

detaileddescription.para: It also updates the Bad Block Table(BBT) in the flash.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the XNandPsu instance.
is the block number.

briefdescription.para: Looks up the device configuration based on the unique device ID/BaseAddress.

detaileddescription.para: The XNandPsu_ConfigTable[] contains the configuration info for each device in the system.

parameterdescription.para: contains the base address of the device

simplesect.para: A pointer to the configuration found or NULL if the specified device ID/BaseAddress was not found. See xnandpsu.h for the definition of XNandPsu_Config.
In XSCT/classic flow, DeviceId is used to look up the device configuration.

detaileddescription.para: This function searches the Bad Block Table(BBT) in flash and loads into the memory based Bad Block Table(BBT).

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the XNandPsu instance.

briefdescription.para: This function searches the BBT in flash.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the XNandPsu instance.
is the BBT descriptor pattern to search.

briefdescription.para: This function scans the NAND flash for factory marked bad blocks and creates a RAM based Bad Block Table(BBT).

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPsu instance.

briefdescription.para: This function converts the Bad Block Table(BBT) read from the flash to the RAM based Bad Block Table(BBT).

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPsu instance.
is the buffer which contains BBT read from flash.

briefdescription.para: This function writes Bad Block Table(BBT) from RAM to flash.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the XNandPsu instance.
is the BBT descriptor to be written to flash.
is the mirror BBT descriptor.

briefdescription.para: This function marks the block containing Bad Block Table as reserved and updates the BBT.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the XNandPsu instance.
is the BBT descriptor pointer.

briefdescription.para: This function updates the primary and mirror Bad Block Table(BBT) in the flash.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the XNandPsu instance.

briefdescription.para: This function initializes the Bad Block Table(BBT) descriptors with a predefined pattern for searching Bad Block Table(BBT) in flash.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPsu instance.

briefdescription.para: This function checks whether a block is bad or not.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the XNandPsu instance.
is the block number.

briefdescription.para: This function calculates ONFI parameter page CRC.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the ONFI parameter page buffer.
is the starting offset in buffer to calculate CRC.
is the number of bytes for which CRC is calculated.

briefdescription.para: Max spare bytes of a NAND flash page of 16K.
This macro sets the bitmask in the register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPsu instance of the controller.
is the register offset.
is the bitmask.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XNandPsu_SetBits(XNandPsu *InstancePtr, u32 RegOffset, u32 BitMask)

briefdescription.para: This macro clears the bitmask in the register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPsu instance of the controller.
is the register offset.
is the bitmask.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XNandPsu_ClrBits(XNandPsu *InstancePtr, u32 RegOffset, u32 BitMask)

briefdescription.para: This macro clears and updates the bitmask in the register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPsu instance of the controller.
is the register offset.
is the bitmask.
is the register value to write.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XNandPsu_ReadModifyWrite(XNandPsu *InstancePtr, u32 RegOffset, u32 Mask, u32 Val)

briefdescription.para: This macro enables bitmask in Interrupt Signal Enable register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPsu instance of the controller.
is the bitmask.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XNandPsu_IntrSigEnable(XNandPsu *InstancePtr, u32 Mask)

briefdescription.para: This macro clears bitmask in Interrupt Signal Enable register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPsu instance of the controller.
is the bitmask.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XNandPsu_IntrSigClear(XNandPsu *InstancePtr, u32 Mask)

briefdescription.para: This macro enables bitmask in Interrupt Status Enable register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPsu instance of the controller.
is the bitmask.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XNandPsu_IntrStsEnable(XNandPsu *InstancePtr, u32 Mask)

briefdescription.para: This macro checks for the ONFI ID.

parameterdescription.para: is the buffer holding ONFI ID

briefdescription.para: Scan the second page for bad block information.
Page offset of Bad Block Table Desc.
Bad Block Table signature offset in page memory.
Bad block Table version offset in page memory.
Block shift value for a block in BBT.
Num of blocks in one BBT entry.
Bad block pattern offset in a page.
Bad block pattern offset in a large page.
Bad block pattern to search in a page.
Block shift mask for a Bad Block Table entry byte.
This macro returns the Block shift value corresponding to a Block.

parameterdescription.para: is the block number.

briefdescription.para: This macro reads the given register.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of controller registers.
is the register offset to be read.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: u32 XNandPsu_ReadReg(u32 BaseAddress, u32 RegOffset)

briefdescription.para: This macro writes the given register.

parameterdescription.para: is the the base address of controller registers.
is the register offset to be written.
is the the 32-bit value to write to the register.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XNandPsu_WriteReg(u32 BaseAddress, u32 RegOffset, u32 Data)

briefdescription.para: ONFI Read Parameter page address cycles.
This file contains the implementation of the interface functions for XNandPsu driver.

detaileddescription.para: The implementation of the XNandPsu driver's static initialization functionality.
This file defines all the ONFI 3.1 specific commands and values.
This file contains the implementation of ONFI specific functions.
This file contains identifiers and low-level macros/functions for the Arasan NAND flash controller driver.
This file contains a configuration table where each entry is a configuration structure for an XNandPsu device in the system.
This file implements the Bad Block Management(BBM) functionality.
This file implements the Bad Block Management (BBM) functionality.
Refer to the header file xnandpsu.h for more detailed information.
This module supports for NAND flash memory devices that conform to the "Open NAND Flash Interface" (ONFI) 3.0 Specification. This modules implements basic flash operations like read, write and erase.

simplesect.para: Driver has been renamed to nandpsu after change in naming convention.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 nm 05/06/2014 First release 2.0 sb 01/12/2015 Removed Null checks for Buffer passed as parameter to Read API's
XNandPsu_Read()
XNandPsu_ReadPage Modified
XNandPsu_SetFeature()
XNandPsu_GetFeature() and made them public. Removed Failure Return for BCF Error check in XNandPsu_ReadPage() and added BCH_Error counter in the instance pointer structure. Added XNandPsu_Prepare_Cmd API Replaced
XNandPsu_IntrStsEnable
XNandPsu_IntrStsClear
XNandPsu_IntrClear
XNandPsu_SetProgramReg with XNandPsu_WriteReg call Modified xnandpsu.c file API's with above changes. Corrected the program command for Set Feature API. Modified
XNandPsu_OnfiReadStatus
XNandPsu_GetFeature
XNandPsu_SetFeature to add support for DDR mode. Changed Convention for SLC/MLC SLC > HAMMING MLC > BCH SlcMlc > IsBCH Removed extra DMA mode initialization from the XNandPsu_CfgInitialize API. Modified
XNandPsu_SetEccAddrSize ECC address now is calculated based upon the size of spare area Modified Block Erase API, removed clearing of packet register before erase. Clearing Data Interface Register before XNandPsu_OnfiReset call. Modified XNandPsu_ChangeTimingMode API supporting SDR and NVDDR interface for timing modes 0 to 5. Modified Bbt Signature and Version Offset value for Oob and No-Oob region. 1.0 kpc 17/6/2015 Added timer based timeout intsead of sw counter. 1.1 mi 09/16/16 Removed compilation warnings with extra compiler flags. 1.1 nsk 11/07/16 Change memcpy to Xil_MemCpy to handle word aligned data access. 1.2 nsk 01/19/17 Fix for the failure of reading nand first redundant parameter page. CR#966603 1.3 nsk 08/14/17 Added CCI support 1.4 nsk 04/10/18 Added ICCARM compiler support. CR#997552. 1.5 mus 11/05/18 Support 64 bit DMA addresses for Microblaze-X platform. 1.5 mus 11/05/18 Updated XNandPsu_ChangeClockFreq to fix compilation warnings. 1.6 sd 06/02/20 Added Clock support 1.6 sd 20/03/20 Added compilation flag 1.8 sg 03/18/21 Added validation check for parameter page. 1.9 akm 07/15/21 Initialize NandInstPtr with Data Interface & Timing mode info. 1.10 akm 10/20/21 Fix gcc warnings. 1.10 akm 12/21/21 Validate input parameters before use. 1.10 akm 01/05/22 Remove assert checks form static and internal APIs. 1.11 akm 03/31/22 Fix unused parameter warning. 1.11 akm 03/31/22 Fix misleading-indentation warning. 1.12 akm 06/27/23 Update the driver to support for system device-tree flow. 1.13 akm 02/13/24 Ensure buffer cache sync. 1.13 akm 02/13/24 Avoid loop counter reset. 1.13 akm 02/13/24 Always wrap page to device size. 1.15 akm 10/28/25 Update IsCacheCoherent logic to include EL3 mode.

detaileddescription.para: See xnandpsu_bbm.h for more details.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 nm 05/06/2014 First release 2.0 sb 01/12/2015 Added support for writing BBT signature and version in page section by enabling XNANDPSU_BBT_NO_OOB. Modified Bbt Signature and Version Offset value for Oob and No-Oob region. 1.1 nsk 11/07/16 Change memcpy to Xil_MemCpy to handle word aligned data access. 1.4 nsk 04/10/18 Added ICCARM compiler support. 1.10 akm 01/05/22 Remove assert checks form static and internal APIs. 1.13 akm 02/13/24 Update BBT writing logic. 1.14 sb 05/28/24 Fix BBT mapping functions. 1.14 sb 05/28/24 Use FlashBbt array of nandpsu structure instead of local Buf array in XNandPsu_WriteBbt() and XNandPsu_ReadBbt(), to avoid stack overflow.

detaileddescription.para: This is similar to the Bad Block Management which is a part of the MTD subsystem in Linux. The factory marked bad blocks are scanned initially and a Bad Block Table(BBT) is created in the memory. This table is also written to the flash so that upon reboot, the BBT is read back from the flash and loaded into the memory instead of scanning every time. The Bad Block Table(BBT) is written into one of the the last four blocks in the flash memory. The last four blocks are marked as Reserved so that user can't erase/program those blocks.

There are two bad block tables, a primary table and a mirror table. The tables are versioned and incrementing version number is used to detect and recover from interrupted updates. Each table is stored in a separate block, beginning in the first page of that block. Only two blocks would be necessary in the absence of bad blocks within the last four; the range of four provides a little slack in case one or two of those blocks is bad. These blocks are marked as reserved and cannot be programmed by the user. A NAND Flash device with 3 or more factory bad blocks in the last 4 cannot be used. The bad block table signature is written into the spare data area of the pages containing bad block table so that upon rebooting the bad block table signature is searched and the bad block table is loaded into RAM. The signature is "Bbt0" for primary Bad Block Table and "1tbB" for Mirror Bad Block Table. The version offset follows the signature offset in the spare data area. The version number increments on every update to the bad block table and the version wraps at 0xff.

Each block in the Bad Block Table(BBT) is represented by 2 bits. The two bits are encoded as follows in RAM BBT. 0'b00 -> Good Block 0'b01 -> Block is bad due to wear 0'b10 -> Reserved block 0'b11 -> Factory marked bad block
While writing to the flash the two bits are encoded as follows. 0'b00 -> Factory marked bad block 0'b01 -> Reserved block 0'b10 -> Block is bad due to wear 0'b11 -> Good Block

The user can check for the validity of the block using the API XNandPsu_IsBlockBad and take the action based on the return value. Also user can update the bad block table using XNandPsu_MarkBlockBad API.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 nm 05/06/2014 First release 2.0 sb 01/12/2015 Added support for writing BBT signature and version in page section by enabling XNANDPSU_BBT_NO_OOB. Modified Bbt Signature and Version Offset value for Oob and No-Oob region. 1.13 akm 02/13/24 Update BBT writing logic.

Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 nm 05/06/2014 First release 1.0 nm 06/02/2014 Changed the copyright to new copyright

detaileddescription.para: See xnandpsu.h for more information.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 nm 05/06/2014 First Release 2.0 sb 11/04/2014 Changed XNANDPSU_ECC_SLC_MLC_MASK to XNANDPSU_ECC_HAMMING_BCH_MASK. 1.7 akm 09/03/20 Updated the Makefile to support parallel make execution.

Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 nm 05/06/2014 First release

Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 nm 05/06/2014 First release 1.4 nsk 04/10/2018 Added ICCARM compiler support.

Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 nm 05/06/2014 First release 1.12 akm 06/27/23 Update the driver to support for system device-tree flow. 1.16 vlt 12/15/25 Update Doxygen comments to include SDT flow details.
